name: Ko Olina
desc: Resort enclave — Four Seasons, Disney Aulani, man-made lagoons, manicured luxury bubble far from real Hawaii.
insider: The four man-made lagoons are legally public — park free at the public parking lot near Lagoon 1 and walk to any of them
Lagoon 4 (Ulua) is the most peaceful and least resort-crowded — it's furthest from the hotels and where locals actually swim
Sunset on the Ko Olina coast is arguably better than Waikiki — completely unobstructed Pacific horizon with no high-rises in the way
Monkeypod Kitchen by Merriman is the best restaurant out here — make reservations, especially for the weekend brunch
Disney's Aulani has a character breakfast that's open to non-guests — book way in advance and bring the kids
The Four Seasons Ko Olina's Noe restaurant has an incredible Italian tasting menu — splurge-worthy for a special occasion
The walking/jogging path along the resort coastline runs about 1.5 miles and is gorgeous at dawn before the resorts wake up
Golf at Ko Olina Golf Club is pricey but the Ted Robinson-designed course with ocean views is one of the best on Oahu
This is a resort bubble — beautiful but completely detached from real Hawaiian life, culture, and community
The beach past Lagoon 4 (sometimes called Secret Beach) is accessible by foot along the coastal path — one of the most secluded stretches of sand in West Oahu
Groceries are resort-priced or nonexistent — stock up at Costco Kapolei or Safeway before heading to your resort
